description: Using the San Francisco UrbanSim HDF5 data at /home/ga/urbansim_projects/data/sanfran_public.h5, analyze residential vacancy rates by zone. Create and execute a Jupyter notebook at /home/ga/urbansim_projects/notebooks/vacancy_analysis.ipynb that computes zone-level vacancy metrics and citywide summary statistics. Classify zones with at least 50 units as 'tight' (vacancy < 0.05), 'healthy' (0.05 to 0.10), or 'soft' (> 0.10), marking zones with fewer units as 'insufficient_data'. Export the zone-level data to /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/vacancy_by_zone.csv (including zone_id, total_units, occupied_units, vacant_units, vacancy_rate, and market_condition). Save a summary JSON to /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/vacancy_summary.json containing citywide_vacancy_rate, num_zones_analyzed, highest_vacancy_zone_id, lowest_vacancy_zone_id, num_tight_zones, num_healthy_zones, and num_soft_zones. Additionally, generate a histogram of vacancy rates with vertical lines at the 5% and 10% thresholds to /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/vacancy_distribution.png.
